purpose: Phase 8 Trust Center for Matrix Reprogrammed. This data defines editorial standards, claim labels, corrections, privacy, source methodology, and no-guilt association boundaries.
principles: A symbol is not proof of conspiracy.
A report is not automatic proof of causation.
Court records, declassified files, datasets, credible reporting, and commentary must remain separate.
Forms must disclose what they collect and why.

pages.title: Source Methodology
pages.label: How the archive reads sources
pages.summary: Matrix Reprogrammed separates court records, declassified files, official datasets, credible reporting, documented association, disputed claims, and symbolic commentary.
sections: Use the strongest available source first.
Preserve the difference between allegation, filing, evidence, finding, settlement, report, and interpretation.
Prefer dated source lanes and public-record routes over viral summaries.

pages.title: Claim Labels
pages.label: How claims are classified
pages.summary: Every claim should carry the right label: confirmed record, court record, credible reporting, documented association, disputed claim, symbolic commentary, or unsupported viral claim.
sections: Confirmed record means the record exists, not that every interpretation of it is true.
Court record means the claim appears in a legal context and must be read by procedural status.
Symbolic commentary is interpretation unless stronger source evidence supports a stronger claim.

pages.title: Association Boundary
pages.label: Names, contacts, photos, flights, meetings, and guilt
pages.summary: A documented association can show contact or proximity. It does not prove knowledge, guilt, control, participation, or criminal intent.
sections: Names in records must be separated by role and source context.
Peripheral contact is not the same as participation.
The archive should never collapse victims, witnesses, employees, contacts, defendants, and convicted persons into one category.

pages.title: Corrections Policy
pages.label: How errors are handled
pages.summary: Corrections are part of the public-record discipline. If a source is misread, a claim is overstated, or a link breaks, the page should be corrected rather than defended.
sections: Correct factual errors quickly when identified.
Prefer specific corrections over vague edits.
Where a claim is uncertain, downgrade the label instead of inflating the evidence.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.label: What forms collect
pages.summary: Matrix Reprogrammed forms may collect name, email, and the reader path voluntarily submitted by the visitor. The purpose is reader routing, not fake scarcity or spam.
sections: Forms collect the fields the visitor submits.
Reader-path notes are used to understand which archive route is relevant.
Do not submit sensitive personal information through public forms.

pages.title: Editorial Standards
pages.label: How dark material stays disciplined
pages.summary: The archive can be dark, forensic, symbolic, and investigative without presenting unsupported claims as facts.
sections: Use direct language without inventing certainty.
Route readers to sources, maps, books, and evidence boundaries for deeper context.
